text:
Location scouting should never be left out! This is an example of how @rubenscott prepares for a shoot 🎥 DP: @ariel_plischuk 

Ruben shares:
✨ *“One thing I never skip: location scouting.
It’s more than just checking out a spot—it’s where the whole vision starts to click. Scouting helps me figure out framing, lens choices, and how to bring the story to life before the camera even rolls.

For me, prep is half the work and all the confidence. Don’t skip it, and definitely don’t skip the scout.”*

Pro tip: apps like Artemis Pro are great for testing lens lengths, framing, and building your shot plan before the shoot day. 👌

text:
Behind the scene 🎬 

Before these visuals appeared on the large public screens in Shibuya, we rebuilt parts of the city in 3D to study the real architectural context, screen positions, and how each piece would actually be seen from the street.

For us, this process was never only about creating content that plays on a screen.
What mattered more was understanding how the work would exist inside the real city — how pedestrians would approach it, how perspective would shift from different viewpoints, how the surrounding buildings would affect depth and scale, and how motion could feel more convincing within the urban environment.

That is why we spent a significant part of the production process simulating real street viewpoints and redesigning each scene around those conditions.
We adjusted perspective, depth, scale, and movement based on how the visuals would be experienced in reality, not just how they looked on a monitor.
The goal was to make each piece feel more naturally connected to the architecture and to create visuals that could extend beyond the screen into the city itself.

This is part of the thinking and simulation behind the final work.

今回の映像を渋谷の街頭大型スクリーンに映し出す前に、私たちは街の一部を3D上で再構築し、建築の形状やスクリーンの位置関係、そして実際に通行人がどのような視点から作品を見るのかを細かく検証しました。

私たちにとって大切なのは、単にスクリーン用の映像を作ることではありません。
むしろ重要なのは、その映像が実際の都市空間の中でどう存在し、どう見えるのかということです。
人がどの方向から近づくのか、見る位置によってパースがどう変わるのか、周囲の建築によって奥行きやスケール感がどう感じられるのか。そうした要素が、最終的な映像の立体感やリアリティに大きく関わってきます。

そのため制作の初期段階から、私たちは実際の街頭視点をシミュレーションしながら、各シーンのパース、奥行き、スケール、動き方をあらためて設計していきました。
モニター上で綺麗に見えることだけではなく、現地で体験したときに、映像が建築や街の流れと自然につながって見えることを目指しています。

今回の投稿では、そうした最終作品の裏側にあるシミュレーションと制作過程の一部を紹介しています。

#chamberlabart #neoshibuyatv #tokyo #behindthescene #makingof

text:
One day. One setup. Total precision. 
Director @boulawayne takes us behind the scenes of his latest project, developed in collaboration with @cinemotionrobotics

Director’s Notes 👇
Total precision using @cinemotionrobotics Ultra system, we combined robot track with a synchronized turntable, to create long motion controlled moves around the car.

A real technical feat, allowing for flawless multipass shots and an extreme level of control over every details.

Thx again to the MoCo Wizard @_ynnb and Dop @julienlascar and Post production @illogicstudios for their talents, creativity and dedication. 

And special thx to @filmmakersworld for their unwavering support to the creator of our community 🙏🙏🙏

text:
a much ‘tidier’ behind the scene set that is able to show up here ☁️ while others we could barely reveal it as those look more like ‘disaster’ scenes 😂

While behind the scenes aren’t just shooting set scene like this but there are lots of prep work before u think we can actually just clicking the shutter button to get a perfect shot 😬

Below explains some about where do photography gets its costs 👀 

From brainstorming ideas, sourcing and choosing the right props, setting up the shooting set by adjusting the lighting, styling the product before we finally get to click on the camera buttons 😆

Not forgetting the post production of it like photo editing and the scene cleanings part lol 😂 It’s so much fun to us but the truth is A LOT OF WORKS!!! We usually ended up with painful bodies after photo-shooting day 🥲

But of course we always try our best to produce artwork that could bring values to our clients’ business and helping them to grow! 💙That’s what we will be proud of and worth all our sweats 💦

text:
Episode 2 Behind the Scene of X-Treme Borneo Challenge 2.0 

Explore the unseen moments from Episode 2 of the Isuzu X-Treme Borneo Challenge 2.0.

Our brave challengers embarked on a remarkable journey from the urban elegance of Kota Kinabalu to the demanding landscapes of the Borneo Safari. These behind-the-scenes photos capture their first foray into the wilderness, showcasing the unmatched capabilities of the Isuzu D-Max. 

Witness the beginning of this extraordinary escapade and the might of the D-Max in action. Be inspired by their determination and share in their adventure.

📸 Plus, don’t forget to participate in our Watch & Win contest to grab your piece of the action with exclusive gifts by following the steps below:
Step 1️⃣ Watch each episode, and answer questions in relation to those episodes.
Step 2️⃣ Then leave your answer and tag 3 of your friends in the comments.
Step 3️⃣ Stay tuned to Isuzu D-Max Facebook to find out if you’re a winner.

Find out more at www.IsuzuXtremeBorneoChallenge.com or click on the Bio Link.

In collaboration with:
Red Springs @ RedSprings, Coleman Malaysia @ Coleman Malaysia and Sabah Tourism Board @ SABAH, Malaysian Borneo

Supported by:
Sabah Four Wheel Drive Association and Tourism Malaysia

These moments are not just snapshots; they are inspirations, capturing the essence of adventure and resilience. Follow us closely on for more adventures.

text:
Here is how we make the famous Hungarian beef goulash. Swipe for video. 
  
Recipe: 
•  1 tbs vegetable oil
• 2 heads of brown onion
• 2 cloves of garlic
• 1 tsp of Piros Arany ( if you don't have no problem you will add paprika later anyway,and we did add a homemade paprika paste as well but you can leave it out) 
• 400g diced beef 
• 1 tsp bicarbonate of soda (it helps to soften the meat but is optinal can be left out) 
• 2.5 tbsp ground paprika
• 500ml water
• 2 Bay leafs 
• Salt and pepper 
• 1 beef stock pot 
• 1 vegetable stock pot
• 100ml red wine such as cabernet sauvignon. 

Method: 
Heat a little oil in a bigger pan than soften the onion and the garlic (if you using Red pepper paste add it now if you not you can skip this step) when the onions are softened add the beef and the bicarbonate of soda (please note that the bicarbonate of soda can foam) and cook until the beef is browned approx 5 minutes than take the pan off from the heat and stir in the ground paprika, it is important to remove the pan from heat because if the paprika burns it will have a bitter unpleasant taste, cook for further 2-3 minutes than add the water, stock pot's and  the seasoning and cook on medium heat for 3-4 hours covered, keep adding water to it as it evaporates always try to keep at least as much water on it that covers the meat, you can set it's consistency at the end of cooking if you like it thick don't add as much water before the end. Approx  15 minutes before the end of the cooking time add the red wine as well. Keep in mind the texture of the goulash should be not to watery but not to thick but it is up to taste really. 

Enjoy. 
.
.
Please note that this recipe is how we make this very versatile stew and it is how we learned from our family.

text:
My curiosity for nature was instilled in me by my father, an avid nature lover. We had a little garden where herbs, vegetables and fruits would be grown in rotation, but there were always a few plants that were permanent fixtures in our green sanctuary. The hibiscus plant was one of them.

The bold petals of the hibiscus flower always stood out against a sea of green, their glorious blooms punctuating the garden in various colours and shades, not unlike an inverted lantern. It is with this flower in mind that Thousand Petals was created.

The key ingredient in our velvety and moussy mask is the roselle flower, a member of the hibiscus family and the more subtle version of the hibiscus flower as we know it. It is more compact in form, but brims with nutrients and medicinal value. Traditionally steeped in hot water to make a tart and refreshing tea, the roselile flower has also made great strides in the world of green chemistry and demonstrated topical skin benefits – specifically its ability to exfoliate ever so gently yet efficiently.

The roselle flower might look pretty, but this beautiful ingredient packs a punch and Thousand Petals is a tribute to this flower I grew up knowing and loving. 

#behindthescenes #howwemakeit #exfoliatingmask 

📷: @tifftiff_studio

text:
We sit down with our lead designer to talk about what the design process looks like for an Evolve board. From initial sketches and prototyping parts, to sending a final board into production, there is many challenges and successes in creating a ground-up design. Full video is on the new eSkate Chat channel (link in story).
•
“Electric forms of transport are an interesting area of development. Aimed at high-end users, the Evolve Carbon Hadean is a premium version of an existing model that showcases a smart use of materials and design construct to reduce weight and bulk. The well-executed upgrade cleverly uses smart technology in the processing (including composite forged carbon fibre) and the electronics (including lithium-Ion battery cells electric motors) to ooze speed and performance. A standout project that ticks all the boxes for good design in this category. Well done.” ~ @gooddesignaus
•

text:
Let’s talk about our pork today!! Like our chicken, it takes us THREE days to make. 😍 Here’s what we do:
-Brine it for 48 hours in a mix of herbs and spices
-Season it with our secret rub
-Stack a mix of pork shoulder and belly on the spit (about a 70/30 mix), and then sear it for 3 hours. Because of how we layer it, you’ll get both shoulder and belly in every dish!
-Then, we steam it in a combi oven for 8 more hours. This makes it super juicy.
That’s it! Our pork - from ZERO to GYRO in just about 3 days. We hope you love it! 💙

text:
💛 Our Moonflower linen fabric was inspired by a combination of; Susie’s garden, where she has a true passion for botanical blooms, and from her everlasting love of Indian motifs.
…
…

A moonflower also known as ‘Belle de Nuit’ translating to Beauty of the Night 🌙 is a sweetly scented white flower which opens in the evening and will close it’s petals by the first morning light.

At dawn the delicately dense packed buds start to shape into a star, and by dusk they have rolled themselves up into a tight safe twist. The form and outline of this trellis flower enthused Susie to create a mottled sketch, which transpired into a simple yet stunning block pattern. Once the pattern was rotated, it created a timeless design; useful for interior decoration, such as #curtains and #blinds.

The scale of the moonflower has allowed us to build on this collection launching a selection of cushions and lampshades which we use alot.. They really are so easy to use everywhere!

text:
From this.... ➡️ To this! ⁠
⁠
Ever wondered how we get all that deep, complex, spicy flavour in our fiery Ginger Beer? 🔥 Well, buckle in for a Fizzy Pop crashcourse, Fizz Fam!⁠
⁠
It all starts with the finest quality root ginger, which we make sure to always buy whole. We press the root on site right here in East London, and steep it to release the natural spicy flavours within. This gives our Ginger Beer a bright, clean taste and natural spice without the need to add chilli or any artificial ingredients! ⁠
⁠
From there, we add freshly squeezed lemon juice, and blend with four different types of dark and light sugars to give a complex and unique flavour! The result? A warming and hearty flavour that's bound to stimulate the palate! ⁠
⁠
𝗗𝗜𝗗 𝗬𝗢𝗨 𝗞𝗡𝗢𝗪?: Our Ginger Beer was THE very first recipe Ed & Robyn made in their kitchen, to sell at local farmers' markets. We've come a long way since then, but our OG (that's Original Ginger 😉) remains refreshingly similar! ⁠
⁠

text:
How is Joie jewellery made?
All of our jewelry is small batch hand made, and we use an age-old technique call lost wax casting.
There are two main processes we use to create our waxes for casting:
1. We take a silicone mould from nature, then do a slip wax pouring of the mould. This is how we create our cedar leaves and sea shell designs, simple and beautiful just the way nature intended the design.

2. The other method is a little newer: after sketching a design, we create first a 2D CAD rendering of the design, then turn the 2D design into a 3D designs and then 3D print the wax. 
I love the CAD process, as I can easily make many changes to a designs, and sometimes create 30 iterations to get it just right. 
I also love exploring seashores and forest looking for that perfect object to cast.
.
.
.
.

text:
BIG NEWS🚨Creatine isn’t just for muscle.
A newly published review analyzed decades of human, animal, and neuroimaging research to examine how creatine interacts with the brain — not hype, not stimulation, but bioenergetics.
The takeaway?

Creatine supports brain energy — especially when demand is elevated.

✔️ Sleep deprivation ✔️ Aging ✔️ Depression ✔️ Neurological stress

In metabolically stable, well-rested young adults? Effects are smaller. Why? Because creatine isn’t a stimulant. It’s energy support.
The research makes one thing clear: Brain impact depends on context, dosing strategy, and duration — even more than muscle does.

Creatine isn’t a shortcut. It’s a biologically essential compound for cellular energy regulation.
The question is no longer if creatine matters for brain health — It’s how to apply it intelligently.

Reference: Creatine Supplementation and the Brain: Have We Put the Cart Before the Horse? Journal of Dietary Supplements DOI: 10.1080/19390211.2026.2616440

Follow us here and as we will continue to send latest Studies.

text:
In @MrBeast’s newest video, contestants compete to be the last person standing inside a fully stocked grocery store with $250,000 on the line. Behind the scenes, our team helped supply the entire meat aisle.

That meant every steak, every cut of chicken, every pork chop, and every piece of seafood in the store came from Good Ranchers.

For us, the moment represents something bigger than product placement. For years, the meat industry has operated in a system where it’s surprisingly difficult for consumers to know where their food actually comes from. Products on grocery store shelves often travel thousands of miles before reaching the case, and origin labeling can be confusing at best.

Good Ranchers was built to change that.

We partner directly with American farms and ranches to deliver beef, chicken, pork, and wild-caught seafood that’s born, raised, and harvested in the United States. Our goal has always been simple: make it possible for families to know exactly where their meat comes from.

MrBeast’s videos regularly reach tens of millions of viewers within days of release, which means a lot of people are about to see something most Americans rarely encounter.

A meat aisle where you actually know what you’re buying.

What you see in that store should not be unusual. It should be the standard.

Until it is, we’ll keep building it ourselves.

text:
For the past few months, our team has been deeply embedded with the @SeminoleBooster, the fundraising, innovation, and development arm of the Florida State @Seminoles.

Last week we started pulling back the curtain on what we've been building behind the scenes. From everything I have seen working with them, I've been blown away with they operate. The way this group thinks about the future of college sports is unlike anything I've seen across the landscape. They're not worried about "keeping up,"  they're building a true, scalable business.

At @NOCAPSports, one of our biggest strengths has been our ability to move fast, pilot intelligently, and adapt to where the industry is heading without overextending. That approach is exactly what's driving results at Florida State. Our work with Seminole Boosters is already generating hundreds of thousands in annual recurring revenue, with multiple millions projected over the next 12 months, and we are only scratching the surface of the strategy we are building with them long term.

But the mission behind the numbers matters just as much. Our focus has been on building sustainable revenue streams for athletic departments - dollars that don't disappear when the donation cycle slows or the NIL market shifts. When a program has a stable, recurring revenue foundation, it creates real capacity to invest in athletes, not just their performance, but their long-term earning potential.

That's what we're building at FSU. What makes this partnership special isn't just the numbers. The Seminole Boosters have given our team full access to embed across everything they're doing, unlocking NOCAP's full capability suite across FSU athletes, fans, sponsors, and donors.

That kind of trust is rare, and we don't take it lightly. Endless appreciation to the entire Seminole team. Let's keep building 🧱 🚀
label: BTS

text:
For years this lived as one of the most talked about ideas in urban water…

Now there is a confirmed site at New York’s Pier 35 funding in place and treatment systems being engineered to operate in the East River.

We have been working behind the scenes along the way. Our team delivered the pilot water treatment system that helped validate the concept and we have now been selected to provide the full scale treatment system as @pluspoolny advances on Manhattan’s Lower East Side!

Why this project stands apart:

💧It draws water directly from the East River filters it through the pool walls and returns it cleaner in real time.

💧A 2024 pilot demonstrated capacity to purify up to one million gallons per day.

💧With 16 million dollars in New York State funding it has shifted from ambitious idea to committed public infrastructure.

Film has played a quiet but powerful role in getting it here. Early @Kickstarter videos carried the idea around the world and rallied thousands of backers. The Swim in the River campaign with @Heineken_US produced by @Tribeca  helped engage a global audience and ultimately helped catalyze a City RFP for an East River swim facility.

At @WEForg ’s WEFTEC this year the World Water Film Festival featured a documentary on the team behind + POOL and hosted a panel on how film can drive connection and action in water. A reminder that infrastructure moves with policy and engineering but also with story.

The first phase will open as a rectangular in river section with the remaining arms of the plus shaped design planned to follow.

I look forward to being on site in New York this summer with my colleagues and the + POOL team as this takes shape in the water. If you’re going to be around, reach out.

-Barron
@watermaxxing_ 

https://t.co/7CJFG6CXTt

text:
It’s Sunday and we hope everyone had a great weekend.

Our team has been heads down working on a lot behind the scenes. It’s difficult to share everything right now, but I want to give the community a few things to be excited about.

We recently started working with an incredible gaming development team. I can’t share specific details yet, but I can say it’s going to be fun, addictive, and full of the kind of adult humor our core holders love.

We also teased a mini game a while back with Cheese fighting pigeons. That is still actively being developed. We have a very unique plan for how it will be released, but I can’t share the details just yet.

On the social side, we had to rebrand our channels to Zero to Hero after TikTok shadow banned us. Apparently they think the logo is a bunny instead of a donkey. Regardless, the rebrand was something that needed to happen anyway. If we ever want to land a deal for an adult animated series, we wouldn’t be able to do that with Web3 or Playboy in the name. The good news is that our Web2 growth has been parabolic and Zero to Hero fits the broader vision very well.

We’re also working on an expansion collection that introduces new characters. One of our animated videos recently went viral, and we realized we need more characters for the adult animated series we’re building. I can’t share more about this yet, but we’re approaching it very strategically and people are going to love what we have planned.

The first limited edition Cheese plushy is currently being produced. I’m hoping to receive the first sample within the next few weeks. Once we confirm the quality meets our standards, we’ll move straight into production. It will be a limited supply at a fair price and available to both our Web2 audience and Web3 holders. There will also be a pool of claimable Cheese traits included, with a few 1/1 Cheese companions.

We’re also sourcing T shirt manufacturers right now and waiting on samples. Once we confirm the quality is where it needs to be, we’ll release our first design in two different colors. And yes, merch will also include claimable traits.

There’s a lot more in the pipeline, but I can’t share additional specifics right now. I’ve said this many times before: when we get quiet, that’s when you should be the most bullish. It means the team is heads down working on things that will increase the success, value, and growth of the brand.

We’ll probably need about another week and a half before I can host a space and take questions. Our team still needs time to meet internally and finalize several things.

text:
How We Make AI Animation Ads

Here's the exact 7-step process we use:

Step 1: Choose your style
Claymation, Pixar, yarn, realistic. Pick one before you touch a prompt. Everything downstream is built around this decision.

Step 2: Research in NotebookLM
Paste the product page URL. Let it learn everything. Features, benefits, the problem it solves. This is your research layer before a single prompt is written.

Step 3: Generate your prompt sequence
Use a prompt inside NotebookLM to build the ad framework. Beginning, middle, end, 60 seconds. Then move it to Claude or ChatGPT and go deeper. Individual image prompts for every shot in the sequence.

Step 4: Generate images in NanoBananaPro
Aim for 10 to 20 images depending on length. Refine with Claude if anything's off. Use Pinterest images as style references to mimic a specific look.

Step 5: Animate in Kling 3.0
Take each image pair. Prompt it to generate a seamless claymation transition between them. 1080p, 7 to 8 second clips. Speed up in post if needed.

Step 6: Voiceover and music
Write the script in the same Claude or ChatGPT session. It already knows the product. Voice in ElevenLabs. Background music in Suno.

Step 7: Stitch in CapCut
Mixed transitions between clips. Layer the voiceover and music. Export at 1080p.

That's a full AI ad. Start to finish.

Most brands are paying $3 to $5k for production to get the same result.

For the full step-by-step process with all prompts like, rt, and comment "ads".

text:
In summary, this is how we make decisions.  Your opponent, assailant, or whatever you choose to call the threat uses the same process.  To win a fight, understand the process, and learn to interupt his/her/its OODA LOOP.

The OODA loop (observe, orient, decide, act loop) is a decision-making model developed by United States Air Force Colonel John Boyd in the early 1970s. He applied the concept to the combat operations process, often at the operational level during military campaigns. It is often applied to understand commercial operations and learning processes.[1] The approach explains how agility can overcome raw power in dealing with human opponents.

The Four Stages of the OODA Loop:

Observe: Gather raw data and information from your environment using all your senses and tools, understanding the current situation.

Orient: This is the most crucial stage; you filter observations through your existing knowledge, experiences, cultural heritage, and mental models to form a meaningful understanding and context.

Decide: Based on your orientation, you choose the best course of action from your available options.

Act: You execute the decision, and this action then becomes new input for the next "Observe" phase, creating a continuous cycle.
